title: Leptilon chinense
taxonomy: Leptilon chinense is a species in the plant family Asteraceae. It is a type of annual or biennial herb. Leptilon chinense is native to the northern regions of the United States and southern Canada. The plant is often found in wetlands, such as marshes and along streams. Leptilon chinense is commonly referred to as Chinese hemp or swamp hemp.
uses: Leptilon chinense, also known as Chinese loosestrife, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine in Asia. The plant's roots and leaves are said to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, it is used to treat various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Its medicinal uses also extend to treating skin conditions, such as acne and eczema, due to its antibacterial properties. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Leptilon chinense as a medicinal plant.

constituents: Leptilon chinense, also known as American pennyroyal, contains various active constituents, including volatile oils such as pulegone and isopulegone, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
